steps.title: Find the passage's central action
steps.content: The pivotal sentence is "This affectionate willingness to fall in with his own plans quite won the old man's heart." David notices his great-uncle's interest in the ship, offers to stay the night, and agrees to go anywhere at any hour — and that consideration directly transforms the meeting into something "almost like old friends." The text is fundamentally about how David's behavior shapes the relationship.

steps.title: Eliminate wrong choices and confirm the answer
steps.content:
Work through each choice against the passage:
  • "It states the reasons why David and his great-uncle Frederick decide to sleep on the ship rather than finding lodging on land.": The text never mentions lodging on land as an alternative, and the sleeping arrangement is a minor example, not the main point. ✗
  • "It showcases how David's flexibility and consideration strengthen his relationship with his great-uncle Frederick.": This matches exactly — the passage shows David's accommodating behavior winning the old man's heart and deepening the bond. ✓
  • "It describes why David and his great-uncle Frederick are excited for their upcoming journey on the ship.": The text provides no details about a planned journey or any excitement about travel; it focuses on the developing relationship. ✗
  • "It contrasts great-uncle Frederick's wary first impressions of David with his ultimate affection toward him.": The meeting begins "auspiciously" and proceeds well from the start — there is no initial wariness. ✗
That matches the correct answer — B is correct.
